pages.name: Continuing the Campaign — the Post-Finale Arc

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 1 (Continuing the Campaign) — p.394-395.

Beyond the Crown (L17–20, optional). The approved postgame (CHG-0003): the main story closes at L17; these seven seeds — three of them developed into a playable arc — carry the table to 20 in the same world they saved.

ARC I — Rulers of Korvosa (L17, intrigue): no monarch in a century of curses. Neolandus runs the restoration and asks the PCs to broker the succession; factions (the noble houses, the church of Abadar's schism, the surviving Arkonas' papers, the freed Gray Maidens) maneuver. Run it as Influence — and let a PC end it on the Crimson Throne, with everything that word now means.

ARC II — Sorshen's Legacy & the Everdawn Pool (L18, dungeon): the Pool's residue stirs what sleeps beneath the city — the pyramid under Castle Korvosa was hers. Destroying the artifact for good is a high-level expedition into Thassilonian deeps; using it, even once, invites Eurythnia's leftovers up the crystal veins.

ARC III — Kazavon Rises (L19–20, the true endgame): the Crown was one relic of seven. Across Avistan the others begin to converge, carried by owners who dream the same dream. If they meet, the dragon is reborn whole. Serithtial knows it; she has been waiting five hundred years to finish this.

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appKazavon000001]{Kazavon (23)}
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial}
ARC III FINALE — Kazavon RebornSevere · 120 XP @ L20

@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appKazavon000001]{KAZAVON} (L23), complete, with whatever army 2d6 weeks of regearing bought him — against one sword. The actor ships his draconic and human forms, the time-stop opening, and his rejuvenation/return logistics. A Severe solo budget at L20 — the regeared army is the dial that pushes the finale to Extreme by design.

Side-seeds (thread into the arcs): Ileosa's Revenge — her soul went to Hell under contract; she can bargain her way back (a devil-army vengeance, or a vampire-return via Pool blood-magic). Lorthact's Plot — the contract's hidden master is the exiled Infernal Duke ruling the Acadamae in secret; Ileosa's soul may be his ticket home, and his enemies pay well. A New Crimson Peril — the Red Mantis remember: the Crimson Citadel raid is 'harder than Scarwall' (and if Cinnabar lives redeemed, they remember her most of all).

The six lost relics are dangling by design. The AP never places the Armor of Skulls, Bound Blade, Howling Horn, Shredskin, Staff of the Slain, or Throne of Nalt — they are Arc III's quest hooks, not an oversight. Seed one per region your table cares about; every owner shares the dream. This conversion deliberately leaves them locationless.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 5 (artifacts) — p.432-436.

The campaign's three great objects, as items: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.serithtialSword1]{Serithtial} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.crownOfFangs0001]{The Crown of Fangs} · and @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck} (Ch.1 items folder), which in the endgame transcends into the Harrow Deck of Many Things — no longer GM hand-waving: the full 54-draw table ships as @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appHarrowDeck001]{a rolltable}, with procedure on @UUID[.jkpIjDTxGAkE2feq]{its own page}. Venster's ghost (Ch.6) may bestow the first draw.

Item links at their recovery scenes: Serithtial is granted at the Sacred Lake islet (Ch.5, H4 — linked there); the Crown is sundered or claimed at the Everdawn Pool (Ch.6, B20 — linked there). Both chapter pages carry the drag-and-drop links.

The @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.3uMAF1feQL5hygty]{Everdawn Pool} (not lootable, mercifully — the item doc is a GM reference): a major artifact of stored life — each charge a day of stolen blood (max 25; 10 remain at the finale; bathing grants bleed-immunity and the Sunken Queen's all-seeing eyes; 5 charges spin up a taniniver; 100 charges in a year buy eternal youth over the donors' corpses). Its destruction wants five impossible bloods and a wish — Arc II's prize.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 5 (Equipment and Magic) — p.430-437.

Every Appendix 5 item the campaign actually hands out, converted (PF1e prices ÷10; magic items keep their identity as PF2e equivalents):

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appRingSplendid1]{Ring of Splendid Security} — Queen Ileosa's ring (now in her inventory).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appWarpaint00001]{Shoanti Warpaint} — all 8 colors with effects (Ch.4's 5-pot grant now has mechanics).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appMantisMask001]{Mask of the Mantis} — Red Mantis issue (embedded in the assassin's gear).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appPlagueMask001]{Plaguebringer's Mask} — why the Queen's Physicians never sicken (Ch.2 evidence).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appDeathCoffer01]{Death's Head Coffer} — the plague's coin-shipment vector (Ch.2).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appSoulJar000001]{Soul Jar} — the cult's reliquary.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appRingImmolate1]{Ring of Immolation} — Cinderlands fire-wreath.
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appSmokeBomb0001]{Smoke Bomb} — Jolistina's signature (now in her inventory).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appUkwarAxe00001]{Ukwar} — the Deadwatcher leader's spirit in her own battleaxe (Ch.5).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.appFoolsLeprosy1]{Fool's Leprosy} — the poison that killed a king, statted as an affliction.

Community statblock adoption also ships: the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.U0hQGyCNfunBoJVT]{Rakshasan Third Eye} (Bahor's, Ch.3), the @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.3uMAF1feQL5hygty]{Everdawn Pool} (filed under Campaign Artifacts), and the eight war-paint colors as individual pots in this folder (the combined doc above stays the Ch.4 grant's mechanics reference). Availability: none of these are on Korvosa's open market; fool's leprosy and the masks are villain-issue only.

pages.name: The Harrow Deck of Many Things

text.content:
Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 3 (harrow deck of many things) — p.428-429.

Endgame artifact (CHG-0010). Near the campaign's end — traditionally after Venster's bestowal or Zellara's farewell — her deck wakes fully. Mechanics ship as @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appHarrowDeck001]{Harrow Deck of Many Things} (54 draws, 1d54).

Procedure:

  • Each PC declares up to 4 draws before touching the deck, then draws one at a time; effects bind only the drawer.
  • The spirit stacks the deck: on an unwanted card, any PC may spend 1 Harrow Point to discard it and redraw (doesn't count against the declared total).
  • Reshuffle everything (including discards) when a new PC begins. After every PC has drawn, the deck is forever quiet — a keepsake, not an artifact.

Table-safety notes: several draws are campaign-grade (The Crows, The Mute Hag, The Lost, The Waxworks); several interact with Serithtial (The Courtesan, The Paladin) or the finale itself (The Twin, The Locksmith, The Owl, The Teamster, The Tyrant). Read the table before offering the deck; the declared-draws rule is the players' only brake.

For the chapter-by-chapter Harrowing (suit draws, Harrow Points, the Chosen), see the Conversion Guide's The Harrowing page — it now carries the chapter→suit table the print sidebar lost.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 6 (NPC Codex) — p.438-463.

Allies & Patrons

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appCressidaKrft1]{Cressida Kroft} — Field Marshal; patron from Ch.1, rebel leader by Ch.6 (the campaign's employer, now an actor)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vencarloOrisini1]{Vencarlo Orisini} — swordmaster, mentor — and Blackjack (Ch.3+; the mantle-handoff mechanics live on the Conversion Guide's Becoming Blackjack page and in the Ch.3 journal)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.neolandusKalep01]{Neolandus Kalepopolis} — the missing seneschal; the lawful lever (Ch.3+)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.triniaSabor00001]{Trinia Sabor} — the framed painter (Ch.1-2) → @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appTriniaSabor01]{Trinia Returned} (L8, Ch.4+; her off-screen Craghawk/ghost-tomb arc and the Training with Trinia retrain ride on the new actor)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.grauSoldado00001]{Grau Soldado} — redeemed watch sergeant (Ch.1-2)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ishaniDhatri0001]{Ishani Dhatri} — Abadaran healer of the plague (Ch.2+)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.aminJalento00001]{Amin Jalento} — rescued young noble (Ch.1, 3, 6)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appThousandBone1]{Thousand Bones} — Skoan-Quah shaman; the Shoanti door (Ch.1, 4)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.krojunEatsWhat01]{Krojun Eats-What-He-Kills} — Sklar-Quah champion; rival, then nalharest (Ch.4)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.akramTruthspeak1]{Truthspeaker Akram} — the last truthspeaker (Ch.4)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.marcusEndrin0001]{Marcus Endrin} — Sable commandant; the great rescue (Ch.3, 4)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.laoriVaus0000001]{Laori Vaus} — cheerful Kuthite; the long redemption (Ch.3, 5)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.shadowcountSial1]{Shadowcount Sial} — Brotherhood of Bones; would-be curate (Ch.4-5) — never without @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appAsyraKyton001]{Asyra}, his kyton eidolon (fights to the death for him; banished, not slain)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sabinaMerrin0001]{Sabina Merrin} — the queen's general; the great defection (Ch.6)
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vensterArabast01]{Venster's ghost} — the kindest informant (Ch.6)
  • Zellara — the harrow-spirit guide (whole campaign; guard her from Scarwall); her deck: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-items.Item.DPDen8gco6rznX9Y]{Zellara's Harrow Deck}

Villains, by Chapter

  • Ch.1 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.RKfT6vJ5guinSBjo]{Gaedren Lamm} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vreegDerroNec001]{Vreeg}
  • Ch.2 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.reinerDavaulus01]{Dr. Davaulus} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.andaisinUrgath01]{Lady Andaisin} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch2RolthLamm0001]{Rolth Lamm} (the Key-Lock Killer; recurring if he escapes) · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ramoskaArkminos1]{Ramoska Arkminos} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jolistinaSusp001]{Jolistina}
  • Ch.3 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.piltsSwastel0001]{Pilts Swastel} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.bahorArkona00001]{Bahor} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.vimandaArkona001]{Vimanda} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sivitDarksphinx1]{Sivit}
  • Ch.4 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinderlander0001]{the Cinderlander} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.cinnabarRedMant1]{Cinnabar} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zenobiaZenderh01]{Zenobia Zenderholm} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kordaitraDesta01]{Kordaitra Destaid}
  • Ch.5 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.zevRavenkaDemi01]{Zev Ravenka} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.belshallamUmbr01]{Belshallam} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kleestadPharma01]{Kleestad}
  • Ch.6 — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.togomorBloatmg01]{Togomor} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.sermignattoDvl01]{Sermignatto} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.kayltanyaRedM001]{Mistress Kayltanya} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.queenIleosa00001]{QUEEN ILEOSA}

Contingencies & Engines

  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.appKazavon000001]{Kazavon} (L23) — the fail-state and Arc III finale; ships with return logistics (2d6 weeks to regear; an Everdawn restoration leaves only fangs on destruction).
  • @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Akaruzug00001]{Akaruzug} (L15) — the soul-engine crucifix construct (Deathhead Vault arc / Scarwall).

Module complete: Chapters 1–6, the subsystems (Harrowing · Reputation/City Tiers · Blood Veil/Epidemic Clock · Respect & Rebellion Points · Becoming Blackjack · Korvosan Guard ranks), the campaign backgrounds, the Appendix 5 items, the Appendix 7 tables, and the postgame arc. Verbatim read-aloud renders only on the GM's machine (local AP.md injection); all mechanics are original PF2e conversion work. Run well.

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 2 (Korvosa and Beyond) — p.396-415.

The city (84 keyed locations, summarized for play): the Heights (Castle Korvosa, the Acadamae — Lorthact's secret), Old Korvosa (Endrin Isle, Old Dock, Fort Korvosa — the Arkonas), North Point (the Bank of Abadar, Citadel Volshyenek, the Longacre Building), Midland (the Shingles, Eel's End), Gray District (the Dead Warrens, the rebel ossuary), South Shore (Carowyn Manor, the noble estates), East Shore (Hellknight Citadel Vraid beyond).

The Shingles & The Vaults

The Shingles: the rooftop shanty-ward — a criminal highway above the Guard's sightline, nested with @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.yPYQC2bfOYmqcfIB]{imps}, the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Zh8awPHA7DZxTboc]{house drakes} that hunt them (and cause nearly as much trouble), stirges in the gutters, and chokers no purge has ever cleared (locals in overshadowed blocks sleep below the second floor for a reason). Random table: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblShingles01]{The Shingles}.

The Vaults: Korvosa's underground has three strata — repurposed Shoanti burial mounds (the mainland's twisting dead-end 'sewers' were tombs first; the Skoan-Quah have not forgotten), purpose-built brick sewage tunnels (the newest, often joining older works behind barred walls), and the Thassilonian deeps — Eurythnia's bones, oldest and worst, with surviving Darklands links. Random table: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblSewers0001]{The Vaults}.

Sites of Interest (V1–V13)

  • V1 Old City Hall ('the Charcoal Palace') — mostly abandoned; a caretaker skeleton crew.
  • V2 Exemplary Execrables — Pilts Swastel's gore-theater (volunteers went missing); burned in the quarantine; its manager re-crowns himself in Ch.3.
  • V3 Temple of Aroden — its three caretakers die of blood veil early; a brutal choker nest by Ch.3.
  • V4 Wreck of the Direption — the false plague ship's grave (Ch.2).
  • V5 Avenue of Arms — 127 identical stone arms along the riverfront, elbows bent away from shore, all sprouting from one buried basalt mass; dig at it and it shrieks and wells black brine. Thassilonian. Leave it alone.
  • V6 Jeggare Museum — curator Mercival Jeggare pays fair coin for Varisian finds (the party's best fence for antiquities).
  • V7 Marbledome — the money-losing opera house.
  • V8 The Gatefoot — a colossal stone foot, all that stands of a statue of Sorshen (rumors of a complex below were never substantiated… Arc II disagrees).
  • V9 Kendall Amphitheater — a sinkhole made a stage by Abadarans and dwarven masons.
  • V10 Eodred's Square — Midland's meeting-plaza (and a statue site for Ileosa's vanity project).
  • V11 Pillar Wall — the 100-foot Thassilonian pillar-row.
  • V12 Great Tomb of Leadership — Gray's guarded crypt of generals.
  • V13 Dead Warrens — the pauper-crypts (Ch.1 Part 3; the rebellion's cellar by Ch.4+).

Proprietors the chapters meet

Eel's End — Devargo Barvasi, the King of Spiders (Ch.1) · Old Fishery — Gaedren Lamm (Ch.1) · All the World's Meat — the Cow Hammer Boys (Ch.1) · Lavender — Vendra Loaggri, the cure-fraud (Ch.2) · Carowyn Manor — the Carowyns, then Jolistina's puppet-show (Ch.2) · Hospice of the Blessed Maiden — Arkona warehouse turned Urgathoan front (Ch.2) · Arkona Palace — 'Glorio' (Ch.3) · Orisini Academy — Vencarlo, until the Red Mantis burn it (Ch.3) · Posh and Turtle — Thaviun Rigulus, wizard-restaurateur (glass floor over Old Tom the sea turtle) · Pestico's Dolls — old Vadid Pestico · Acadamae — headmaster Toff Ornelos (gates barred all campaign) · Bank of Abadar — Archbanker Darb Tuttle (fights the free-healing schism; Ishani's superior) · Grand Cathedral of Pharasma — Keppira d'Bear (secret rebel landlord under martial law) · Jeggare Light — a keeper who is no longer alive, exactly (the cult's penanggalen lamplighter).

City state & markets

City state-tracking: run Korvosa's condition by the Conversion Guide's Reputation & City Tiers page — it now ships the concrete tier table (Baseline L14 → Unrest L11 → Plagued L10 → Martial Law L8 → Anarchy/black-market L11, with purchase caps and hireling casting per tier), driven by the Epidemic Clock (Blood Veil page) and the chapters' events. The Rumor Mill ships as @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblRumors0001]{a scaffold rolltable} (@Check[type:diplomacy|dc:15] to @Check[type:diplomacy|dc:25] by tier).

The hinterlands & beyond

Near: Harse (Blackbird Ranch; ferry-town where fugitive PCs are recognized fast — @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblHinter0001]{Hinterlands table}), Janderhoff (the dwarven Sky Citadel that shelters Vencarlo and Neolandus, start of Ch.4), Citadel Vraid (the Hellknights — mercenary allies who leave when the pay does, Ch.2), Kaer Maga (the cliff-city resupply), Veldraine and Palin's Cove (the navy and the siege-engine yards), Melfesh, Baslwief, Biston, Sirathu, Abken (the holdings).

Far: the Cinderlands (Ch.4 — travel hazards now real: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.appEmberstorm001]{Emberstorm} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.appCinderCone001]{Cinder Cone Bombardment} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-hazards.Actor.appCinderHeat001]{Heat & Thirst}; encounters: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblCinder0001]{Cinderlands table}; flora worth knowing: flask trees store water, galtroot becomes the Shoanti's 'barbarian chew,' ember poppies sprout after burns), Belkzen (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblBelkzen001]{Belkzen table} — Ch.5 assumes magical transit), the Mushfens (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblMushfen001]{Mushfens table} — Ch.6's swamp road), the Jeggare River (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblJeggare001]{River table}).

Source: CotCT (2016 HC), Appendix 7 (Bestiary) — p.464-477.

Random encounter rules: 20% chance per day of travel or night of rest (GM's dial). The three Korvosa tables add the city's current Danger modifier (+10 Unrest/Plagued · +20 Martial Law · +30 Anarchy) to the d% roll — the nasty results live above 100; halve the roll to skew safer. Scarwall (Ch.5) uses its own chapter rules.

The eight tables: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblShingles01]{The Shingles} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblStreets001]{Korvosa Streets} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblSewers0001]{The Vaults (Sewers)} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblJeggare001]{Jeggare River} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblCinder0001]{The Cinderlands} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblHinter0001]{Korvosan Hinterlands} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblBelkzen001]{Hold of Belkzen} · @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblMushfen001]{The Mushfens} — plus @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-rolltables.RollTable.appTblRumors0001]{the Rumor Mill}.

The nine new monsters → where they live now

  • House drake — official PF2e print: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.Zh8awPHA7DZxTboc]{House Drake} (a CG imp-hunter; 7th-level CG casters may take one as a familiar).
  • Dream spider — official PF2e print: @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.4SfnQzORMdmBsp49]{Dream Spider} (its venom distills into the drug shiver — Ch.1's economy).
  • Drain spider / shingle spider — module actor: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.1M94tm3wnSRGcTG5]{Drain Spider} (the shingle variant is the same beast in a chimney).
  • Chained spirit — module actor: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.mithrodarChain01]{Mithrodar} (Ch.5's curse-heart; the anchor mechanics live on his sheet).
  • Danse macabre — community statblock (creature, L15): @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.jZdFkFjqvA181dBP]{Danse Macabre} (Ch.5).
  • Red reaver — module actor: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.redReaver0000001]{Red Reaver} (beauty-fascinated siege-beast).
  • Akaruzug — new appendix actor: @UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.ch6Akaruzug00001]{Akaruzug} (the soul-engine crucifix).
  • Havero — tentacles only, by design: the full CR-21 horror is never fought; Ch.4/Ch.6 stage only its reaching limbs (@UUID[Compendium.cotct-pf2e-conversion.cotct-actors.Actor.haveroTentacle01]{Havero Tentacle}). Severing tentacles buys passage — the body behind the wall is a reason to leave, and the conversion keeps it that way deliberately.
  • Pharmakos — the god-cursed apostate-guardian has no module actor (its one cameo is off the campaign's spine); improvise from @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core-2.Actor.TQtISRsI3FI2ddBR]{Mobogo} (Elite) with constant agony (immune to pain), an acid-blood breath cone, and Strength-sapping claws if your table goes looking.

PF2e substitutions inside the tables (no official print exists): yrthak → Thunderbird · bhuta → Elite Specter · dragonne → Elite leonine Manticore · ash giant → Cave Giant · ratling → speaking Elite Giant Rat · rat king → crowned Elite Rat Swarm · devilfish → Elite Reef Octopus · arsinoitherium → Woolly Rhinoceros · NPC-Codex humans → reflavored module NPCs. Everything else links the official Bestiary/Monster Core.
